name: Za Jiang Mein
recipeInstructions:
Garnish suggestions: blanched bean sprouts, fresh coriander leaves, cucumber slices, and vinegar and chili oil mixed in a 2:1 ratio.
Heat the oil in a wok over medium heat. Add the pork and stir-fry until browned. Break up the pork into small granules with a spatula or wooden spoon.
Add the bean sauce and sugar to the pork mixture. Stir and cook, stirring frequently, until the sauce is piping hot and well combined.
Turn off the heat. Prepare the noodles by bringing a large pot of water to a boil. Add the fresh Chinese noodles and cook according to package instructions until al dente (usually about 4 minutes).
Drain the cooked noodles thoroughly and transfer them to a serving dish. Drizzle with a small amount of sesame oil.
Reheat the pork sauce in a separate pan over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until warmed through. Stir in the chopped scallions.
Turn off the heat and garnish with sesame oil as desired. Serve the stir-fried noodles topped with the warm pork sauce and any or all of the garnishes.
